title: Detailed step-by-step protocol for copper doping of synthetic hydroxyapatite thin films by plasma-assisted pulsed laser deposition (PAPVD)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"hfj8t","text":"This protocol described the fabrication of copper-doped hydroxyapatite (HAp-Cu) thin films on Si (100) substrates using a plasma-assisted pulsed laser deposition (PAPVD) system. Copper was incorporated in situ through intermittent ablation of metallic copper strips placed on the surface of a sintered hydroxyapatite (HAp) target. The protocol included: (i) synthesis of HAp powder; (ii) compaction and sintering of dense HAp targets; (iii) substrate cleaning; (iv) modification of the target with copper strips; (v) deposition parameters and sequence; and (vi) post-deposition handling and verification points.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"bk69w","text":"- Confirm the availability and proper functioning of the PAPVD chamber, pumps, pressure gauges, and laser safety interlocks.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"117jq","text":"- Clean all glassware and reaction vessels with deionized (DI) water. Avoid contamination by phosphates from detergents, especially in glassware intended for synthesis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bl4yt","text":"- Calibrate the pH meter using two-point calibration and check the temperature probe, if applicable.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"m4qnf","text":"- Prepare the work plan for handling hydrofluoric acid (HF): certified fume hood, PPE, spill kit, and calcium gluconate gel available.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"coqzf","text":"- Provide clean, labeled containers for substrates and deposited samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"31so9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"faorj","text":"Reference times: HAp synthesis (2 h addition + 48 h aging + 1–2 h washing/filtration + 24 h drying); target fabrication (pressing: 15 min; sintering: 20 h, including cooling); substrate cleaning (1 h); deposition run (1 h, including pumping and cooling)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"nzqg1","text":"- All procedures involving hydrofluoric acid (HF) were performed inside an HF-certified fume hood using compatible personal protective equipment (double gloves, face shield, safety goggles, and chemical-resistant apron). Calcium gluconate gel was kept readily available for emergency use, and institutional emergency procedures were followed.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9hqcb","text":"- The Nd:YAG pulsed laser (532 nm) was operated under Class 4 laser safety controls (interlocks, beam enclosure whenever possible, and certified protective eyewear for 532 nm). Only trained personnel handled the laser system.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"firym","text":"- Furnace operation up to 1150 °C was conducted using thermal gloves and appropriate burn-prevention measures.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"lbifp","text":"- Vacuum and high-voltage operations were carried out following standard vacuum safety practices (view-port protection and controlled venting) and equipment-specific electrical safety regulations.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cgjr9","text":"- Waste solutions containing nitrates/ammonium, acetone, and HF residues were collected separately in designated containers. Incompatible waste streams were not mixed.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

homogenization.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"0qrqr","text":"- Hydraulic press + 26 mm die Uniaxial pressing at 200 MPa for 10-15 equivalent).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"lcd1f","text":"- PAPVD/PLD chamber Base pressure \u00003c5 x 10^-6 mbar; target rotation and scanning (rastering).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ad7i4","text":"- Nd:YAG laser 532 nm (2nd harmonic), pulses ~6 ns; 10 Hz; 85 mJ per steps: Figure 4. Representative image of vacuum system, laser configuration, and radial Cu-strip arrangement. Figure 5. Representative image of vacuum system, laser configuration, and radial Cu-strip arrangement.
Cool the substrates inside the chamber for at least 30 min.
Slowly vent the chamber to atmospheric pressure using dry nitrogen to limit moisture adsorption on the fresh films.
Remove the samples with clean tweezers and store them in sealed containers (Petri dishes or sample boxes) until characterization.

A visual inspection was performed for interference colors or visible coating on the bare Si, indicating film formation. Additionally, surface and quantization techniques were used in this case, energy dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (EDS) for the detection of Ca, P, O, and Cu peaks (Si from the substrate) and scanning electron microscopy (SEM) on the continuous coating with nano to submicrometric particles typical of PLD at room temperature. Finally, a structural analysis with XRD to identify hydroxyapatite peaks without prominent crystalline peaks of Cu/CuO (Cu may be amorphous or below the detection limit).

Problem: Target cracks during sintering Probable cause: Thermal stress; rapid ramps; inhomogeneous green compact Action/mitigation: Ensure uniform pressing; follow slow ramps (3 °C/min up to 700 °C and 1 °C/min up to 1150 °C); allow to cool with the furnace closed.

Problem: No film observed / very low deposition rate Probable cause: Laser misalignment; low fluence; shutter closed; target out of focus Action/mitigation: Realign the beam; confirm energy on target; measure spot size; check lens position and window cleanliness.

Problem: Unstable pen / excess particles Probable cause: Localized perforation; insufficient rastering; contaminated target surface Action/mitigation: Increase rastering coverage; verify rotation at 10 rpm; gently polish/clean the surface; reduce energy per pulse if necessary.

Problem: Copper is not detected by EDS. Probable cause: The strips are not ablated (poor contact/geometry); few strips; short run Action/mitigation: Improve contact; reposition so that the beam intermittently intersects Cu during rotation; increase the number of strips within the study design; extend the deposition time consistently.

Problem: Low adhesion/detachment Probable cause: Substrate contamination; rust regrowth; handling Action/mitigation: Minimize the time between HF bath and loading; do not touch the active surface; consider in situ cleaning with plasma/ions if available.

Problem: Pressure peaks or surges Probable cause: Leaks; adhesive outgassing; contaminated chamber Action/mitigation: Check for leaks; bake/clean the chamber; avoid adhesives that are not vacuum compatible; ensure adequate pumping and throttling.
creator: LEONARDO BOHORQUEZ SANTIAGO
